title: collect_two_string_lists uses Vec to index [0],[1]
description: evaluator.rs:524-536 — collect_two_string_lists builds a Vec<Vec<&str>> from the form slice and then reads lists.first() and lists.get(1). The only purpose of the intermediate Vec is to hold two elements that are then immediately destructured. Idiomatic Rust expresses this as an iterator: filter the forms to string-list entries, call next() twice, and return a pair — zero heap allocation and clearer intent. The current pattern allocates a Vec<Vec<&str>> on every call to eval_header_test, eval_address_test, and eval_envelope_test.
